title: swap opcode — register swap (OTP 27+)
purpose: The `swap` opcode (opcode 169) atomically exchanges the values of two registers. The OTP 27+ compiler emits `swap` frequently as an optimization — it replaces a 3-instruction `move` sequence (A→tmp, B→A, tmp→B) with a single instruction. Without `swap`, any code compiled with OTP 27+ hits `UnsupportedOpcode(169)`. Since beamr targets OTP 27 bytecode, this is critical.
task: Wire the existing `Instruction::Swap { left, right }` variant (already decoded by the loader) into the opcode dispatch table and implement the handler in `core.rs`.

requirements.title: Opcode dispatch wiring
requirements.spec: Add `Instruction::Swap` to the dispatch table in `opcodes/mod.rs`, routing to a handler in `core.rs`.
acceptance: `Instruction::Swap { left, right }` is matched in the dispatch table
No `UnsupportedOpcode(169)` error when executing modules containing `swap`

requirements.title: swap handler implementation
requirements.spec: Implement `swap` in `core.rs`. The instruction reads both operands, then writes each value to the other's location.
acceptance: `swap(process, module, left, right)` exchanges the values of two registers
Both operands are read via `core::read_term()` before either write
Both values are written via `core::write_term()` after both reads (no clobber)
Works for X-X, Y-Y, and X-Y register combinations
Test: Y0=atom(hello), X0=atom(world). swap(Y0, X0). Y0=atom(world), X0=atom(hello).
Test: swap(X0, X0) is a no-op (same register)

boundaries: SHALL NOT allocate heap space — swap copies 64-bit Term values only (same as move)
SHALL NOT add a temporary register — read both values, then write both
Note: core.rs is at 583 lines (over 500-line guideline). swap adds ~15 lines. Full core.rs split is a separate concern.
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
